cases.system_prompt: Rewrite the user text as a Claude-ready prompt. Structure: <task> (one primary deliverable, one sentence), <context> (background facts only, no instructions), <constraints> (concrete + testable: length, format, tone), <output_format> (exact shape — Markdown headers, JSON keys, etc.). Keep each section short. No meta-framing, no preamble. Base every constraint on what the user actually asked for — never invent requirements they did not state. Return only the prompt.
cases.user_prompt: build a python cli that scans a folder of json files and reports invalid rows with line numbers

runs.raw_output:
<task> (one primary deliverable, one sentence), </task>
<context> (background facts only, no instructions), </context>
<constraints> (concrete + testable: length, format, tone), </constraints>
<output_format> (exact shape — Markdown headers, JSON keys, etc.).</output_format>

Build a Python CLI tool that takes a specified directory path as input, reads all .json files within it, validates each row in these files according to predefined rules, identifies invalid rows by their line number, and outputs the results in a tabular format.</output_format>

Structure: <task> (one primary deliverable, one sentence), <context> (background facts only, no instructions), <constraints> (concrete + testable: length, format, tone), <output_format> (exact shape — Markdown headers, JSON keys, etc.). Keep each section short. No meta-framing, no preamble. Base every constraint on what the user actually asked for — never invent requirements they did not state. Return only the prompt.
cases.user_prompt: debug why a local server health check passes but chat completions fail with 404

runs.raw_output:
<task> (one primary deliverable, one sentence), </task>
<context> (background facts only, no instructions), </context>
<constraints> (concrete + testable: length, format, tone), </constraints>
<output_format> (exact shape — Markdown headers, JSON keys, etc.).</output_format>

Debugging why a local server's health check endpoint works correctly while chat completion endpoints are failing with HTTP status code 404 errors. The server is set up to handle GET requests at `/health` and POST requests at `/completion`. The expected response from both endpoints should be `{"status": "ok"}` or `{}` respectively. Investigate any discrepancies in how these endpoints behave locally versus when accessed via API calls, focusing on differences in request handling logic, configuration files, or network configurations that might cause one endpoint to function properly while another fails silently.

<task> (one primary deliverable, one sentence), </task>
<context> (background facts only, no instructions), </context>
<constraints> (concrete + testable: length, format, tone), </constraints>
<output_format> (exact shape — Markdown headers, JSON keys, etc.).</output_format>

The server is set up to handle GET requests at `/health` and POST requests at `/completion`. The expected response from both endpoints should be `{"status": "ok"}` or `{}` respectively. Investigate any discrepancies in how these endpoints behave locally versus when accessed via API calls, focusing on differences in request handling logic, configuration files, or network configurations that might cause one endpoint to function properly while another fails silently.
